task: Pivot /tmp/clive/sales.csv from long to wide format. Rows are products, columns are months, values are revenue. Write the pivoted table to /tmp/clive/result.csv.

initial_state.setup: echo 'product,month,revenue' > /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget A,Jan,5000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget A,Feb,6000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget A,Mar,5500' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget B,Jan,3000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget B,Feb,3500' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget B,Mar,4000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget C,Jan,8000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget C,Feb,7500' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv && echo 'Widget C,Mar,9000' >> /tmp/clive/sales.csv

success_criteria.check: test -f /tmp/clive/result.csv && head -1 /tmp/clive/result.csv | grep -qi 'Jan' && head -1 /tmp/clive/result.csv | grep -qi 'Mar' && test $(wc -l < /tmp/clive/result.csv | tr -d ' ') -eq 4

task: Flatten a nested JSON structure at /tmp/clive/nested.json into a flat CSV. Nested keys should become dot-separated column names (e.g., 'address.city'). Write to /tmp/clive/result.csv.

Create a SQLite database from /tmp/clive/employees.csv, then query: 'SELECT department, COUNT(*) as count, AVG(salary) as avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department ORDER BY avg_salary DESC'. Write results to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup: echo 'id,name,department,salary' > /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '1,Martin,Engineering,85000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '2,Kiu,Engineering,92000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '3,Sarah,Product,78000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '4,Tom,Sales,65000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '5,Lisa,Engineering,88000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '6,Alex,Sales,70000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv && echo '7,Nina,Product,82000' >> /tmp/clive/employees.csv

task: Convert the XML file at /tmp/clive/data.xml to JSON format. Write the result to /tmp/clive/result.json.

initial_state.setup:
cat > /tmp/clive/data.xml << 'XML'
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<employees>
  <employee id="1">
    <name>Martin Treiber</name>
    <email>martin@ikangai.com</email>
    <role>CEO</role>
  </employee>
  <employee id="2">
    <name>Kiu Chen</name>
    <email>kiu@ikangai.com</email>
    <role>CTO</role>
  </employee>
</employees>
XML

success_criteria.check: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/clive/result.json')); assert 'Martin' in str(d) and 'Kiu' in str(d)"

task: Deduplicate records in /tmp/clive/records.csv by email address, keeping the most recent entry (by date column). Write deduplicated records to /tmp/clive/result.csv.

initial_state.setup: echo 'date,name,email,company' > /tmp/clive/records.csv && echo '2026-01-15,Kiu Chen,kiu@ikangai.com,ikangai' >> /tmp/clive/records.csv && echo '2026-03-20,Kiu C.,kiu@ikangai.com,ikangai UG' >> /tmp/clive/records.csv && echo '2026-02-10,Sarah M,sarah@techstart.de,TechStart' >> /tmp/clive/records.csv && echo '2026-04-01,Sarah Mueller,sarah@techstart.de,TechStart GmbH' >> /tmp/clive/records.csv && echo '2026-03-15,Tom Baker,tom@globalfin.com,GlobalFin' >> /tmp/clive/records.csv

task: Analyze the Apache-style access log at /tmp/clive/access.log. Report: total requests, unique IPs, top 5 requested paths, requests per HTTP status code, and peak hour. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup: echo '192.168.1.1 - - [09/Apr/2026:10:15:30 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1234' > /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '10.0.0.5 - - [09/Apr/2026:10:16:45 +0000] "GET /api/users HTTP/1.1" 200 567' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '192.168.1.1 - - [09/Apr/2026:10:17:00 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 401 89' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '172.16.0.3 - - [09/Apr/2026:11:20:10 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1234' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '10.0.0.5 - - [09/Apr/2026:11:25:00 +0000] "GET /api/users HTTP/1.1" 200 567' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '192.168.1.1 - - [09/Apr/2026:12:30:00 +0000] "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 404 0' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '172.16.0.3 - - [09/Apr/2026:12:35:00 +0000] "GET /api/users HTTP/1.1" 500 45' >> /tmp/clive/access.log && echo '10.0.0.5 - - [09/Apr/2026:13:00:00 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1234' >> /tmp/clive/access.log

task: Join two CSV files on a common key. /tmp/clive/orders.csv (order_id, customer_id, amount) and /tmp/clive/customers.csv (customer_id, name, email). Write the joined result to /tmp/clive/result.csv with columns: order_id, customer_name, email, amount.

initial_state.setup: echo 'order_id,customer_id,amount' > /tmp/clive/orders.csv && echo '1,C001,250' >> /tmp/clive/orders.csv && echo '2,C002,180' >> /tmp/clive/orders.csv && echo '3,C001,320' >> /tmp/clive/orders.csv && echo '4,C003,450' >> /tmp/clive/orders.csv && echo 'customer_id,name,email' > /tmp/clive/customers.csv && echo 'C001,Kiu Chen,kiu@ikangai.com' >> /tmp/clive/customers.csv && echo 'C002,Sarah Mueller,sarah@techstart.de' >> /tmp/clive/customers.csv && echo 'C003,Tom Baker,tom@globalfin.com' >> /tmp/clive/customers.csv

task: Compute descriptive statistics for /tmp/clive/measurements.csv: mean, median, standard deviation, min, max, and quartiles (Q1, Q3) for the 'value' column. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup: echo 'timestamp,value' > /tmp/clive/measurements.csv && for v in 23.5 25.1 22.8 26.3 24.7 28.0 21.9 25.5 23.2 27.1 24.0 26.8 22.5 25.9 24.3; do echo "2026-04-09T$(printf '%02d' $((RANDOM % 24))):00:00,$v"; done >> /tmp/clive/measurements.csv

task: Compare two JSON config files (/tmp/clive/config_old.json and /tmp/clive/config_new.json) and report: added keys, removed keys, and changed values. Write the diff report to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Convert /tmp/clive/data.csv to a properly formatted markdown table. Handle alignment (numbers right-aligned, text left-aligned). Write to /tmp/clive/result.md.

initial_state.setup: echo 'name,role,salary,start_date' > /tmp/clive/data.csv && echo 'Martin Treiber,CEO,95000,2024-01-15' >> /tmp/clive/data.csv && echo 'Kiu Chen,CTO,92000,2024-03-01' >> /tmp/clive/data.csv && echo 'Sarah Mueller,PM,78000,2024-06-15' >> /tmp/clive/data.csv

task: Convert a YAML config file to JSON. Read /tmp/clive/config.yaml and write equivalent JSON to /tmp/clive/result.json.

initial_state.setup:
cat > /tmp/clive/config.yaml << 'YML'
server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  workers: 4
database:
  host: db.ikangai.com
  port: 5432
  name: clive_prod
logging:
  level: info
  format: json
YML

success_criteria.check: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/clive/result.json')); assert d['server']['port']==8080; assert d['database']['host']=='db.ikangai.com'"

task: Extract all email addresses and phone numbers from /tmp/clive/document.txt using regex. Write unique emails and phone numbers (separately labeled) to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup:
cat > /tmp/clive/document.txt << 'DOC'
Please contact Kiu Chen at kiu@ikangai.com or call +49 176 1234567.
For billing questions, email billing@ikangai.com or accounting@ikangai.com.
Sarah can be reached at sarah@techstart.de, phone: +49-170-9876543.
Our main office: +49 (89) 12345678, info@ikangai.com
DOC

task: Analyze time series data from /tmp/clive/metrics.csv. Detect: trend direction (up/down/flat), calculate 7-day moving average, find the peak and trough dates. Write analysis to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup: echo 'date,value' > /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-01,100' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-02,105' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-03,98' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-04,112' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-05,108' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-06,115' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-07,120' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-08,118' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv && echo '2026-04-09,125' >> /tmp/clive/metrics.csv

task: Validate /tmp/clive/import.csv: check for missing required fields (name, email), invalid email formats, duplicate emails, and inconsistent date formats. Write a validation report with error counts to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

initial_state.setup: echo 'name,email,date,amount' > /tmp/clive/import.csv && echo 'Kiu Chen,kiu@ikangai.com,2026-04-01,500' >> /tmp/clive/import.csv && echo ',missing@email.com,2026-04-02,300' >> /tmp/clive/import.csv && echo 'Bad Email,not-an-email,04/03/2026,200' >> /tmp/clive/import.csv && echo 'Kiu Chen,kiu@ikangai.com,2026-04-04,400' >> /tmp/clive/import.csv && echo 'Good Record,good@test.com,2026-04-05,' >> /tmp/clive/import.csv

task: Use jq to transform /tmp/clive/api_response.json: extract only users with active status, rename 'full_name' to 'name', add a computed field 'domain' extracted from email. Write to /tmp/clive/result.json.

success_criteria.check: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/clive/result.json')); assert len(d)==2; assert all('name' in r and 'domain' in r for r in d); assert d[0]['domain']=='ikangai.com'"
